entries.d: A tall piece of furniture where you hang your clothes
entries.s: I put my dresses and jackets in the _____.

entries.d: The place where you sleep at night
entries.s: I go to _____ at nine o'clock every night.

entries.d: A small table next to where you sleep
entries.s: I put my phone on the _____ before I go to sleep.

entries.d: A soft covering on the floor that you walk on
entries.s: We have a new blue _____ covering the whole floor of the living room.

entries.d: A cold box in the kitchen where you keep food fresh
entries.s: The milk is in the _____ next to the yoghurt and cheese. It stays cool there.

entries.d: A machine in the kitchen where you heat food
entries.s: Be careful near the _____ — it has four burners and is very hot when you cook on it.

entries.d: A piece of cloth that hangs over a window
entries.s: Close the long _____ in front of the window please. The sun is too bright.

entries.d: The place in the kitchen where you wash dishes
entries.s: Put the dirty plates in the kitchen _____ so we can wash them.

entries.d: A piece of furniture with doors where you keep cups and food
entries.s: The glasses are in the wooden _____ on the wall above the kitchen counter.

entries.d: A flat piece of furniture with legs where you eat or work
entries.s: We eat dinner at the _____ every evening.

entries.d: A piece of furniture you sit on, with a back and four legs
entries.s: Please sit on the small wooden _____ with four legs and a back, and wait for your turn.

entries.d: A long soft seat for two or more people in the living room
entries.s: The whole family of four sits together on the big soft _____ to watch TV in the living room.

entries.d: A big soft seat with sides where you rest your arms
entries.s: Grandpa always reads his book in his favourite _____.

entries.d: A thing that gives you light when it is dark
entries.s: Turn on the desk _____ please. I cannot see my book in the dark corner.

entries.d: A metal thing on the wall that makes the room warm
entries.s: My cat loves to sleep next to the warm _____ in winter when the heating is on.

entries.d: A small soft cover you put on the floor
entries.s: There is a nice small _____ on the floor in front of the fireplace.

entries.d: The top part of a building that keeps the rain out
entries.s: A bird is sitting on the _____ of our house.

entries.d: The flat side of a room or building
entries.s: I put a poster of my favourite band on my bedroom _____.

entries.d: A glass opening in a room that lets you see outside
entries.s: Open the _____ please. It is too hot in here.

entries.d: Steps inside a building that take you up or down
entries.s: Be careful on the marble _____ in the main hall. The steps are slippery.

entries.d: A room under the ground floor of a house
entries.s: We keep old boxes and bottles in the dark _____ below the ground floor.

entries.d: A home on wheels that you can pull with a car
entries.s: They live in a _____ near the lake. It has wheels and they can pull it with their car.

entries.d: A small room built in a tree to play in
entries.s: We read books and played games in our _____ up in the big oak tree.

entries.d: Long sticks or poles that hold something up above the ground
entries.s: In some countries, houses stand on _____ over the water.

entries.d: A tall plant that grows near water
entries.s: The birds hide in the _____ at the edge of the lake.

entries.d: A piece of land with water all around it
entries.s: We took a boat to a small _____ in the lake.

entries.d: The flat surface you walk on outside
entries.s: The apple fell from the high tree branch down to the _____ outside the house.

entries.d: A word that means it belongs to us
entries.s: That house is not theirs. It is _____.

entries.d: A word that means it belongs to them
entries.s: Our garden is small, but _____ is very big.

entries.d: A word that means it belongs to me
entries.s: This blue bag is _____. I bought it last week.

entries.d: A word that means it belongs to her
entries.s: That is not my jacket. It is _____.

entries.d: A word that means it belongs to him
entries.s: Tom forgot _____ lunch box at home today.

entries.d: A word that means it belongs to you
entries.s: Is this pencil _____? It has your name on it.

entries.d: Things like tables, chairs, and beds in a room
entries.s: We need new _____ for the living room — a sofa, a table, and some chairs.

entries.d: A question word to ask who owns something
entries.s: _____ bag is this? Someone left it in the classroom.

entries.d: A person from the USA, or something from that country
entries.s: My pen pal is _____. She lives in New York.

entries.d: The area in the middle of the biggest continent
entries.s: Some families in _____ live in special round tents.

entries.d: The power that makes lights, TVs, and fridges work
entries.s: We had no _____ last night because of the storm.

entries.d: To stay on top of water without going down
entries.s: The toy boat _____ on the water in the bathtub.

entries.d: Can be moved from one place to another
entries.s: The desk has wheels so it is easy to move around the room. It is very _____.

entries.d: Below or under something
entries.s: The cat is sleeping _____ the kitchen table, right below the chairs.

entries.d: To take something from one place to another
entries.s: We used a big van to _____ the old furniture.

entries.d: A piece of cloth you hang between two trees and lie in
entries.s: I love reading in the _____ in our garden.

entries.d: A soft white material used to make clothes
entries.s: This T-shirt is made of _____ and feels very soft.

entries.d: Material made from animal skin, used for shoes and bags
entries.s: My dad has a brown _____ jacket. It is made from animal skin.

entries.d: What something is made of
entries.s: What _____ is your school bag made of — leather, cotton, or plastic?

entries.d: A hard shiny thing like iron or gold
entries.s: The spoon is cold and made of _____. It is shiny and heavy.

entries.d: A design with shapes, lines, or colours on something
entries.s: My new dress has a flower _____ on it.

entries.d: Only one colour, with no design on it
entries.s: She wore a _____ white T-shirt with jeans.

entries.d: A light, cheap material used to make many things
entries.s: We should use less _____ to help the environment.

entries.d: A small area of water, smaller than a lake
entries.s: There are frogs in the _____ in our garden.

entries.d: A place where you sit, like a chair or bench
entries.s: Please take a _____ and wait for the doctor.

entries.d: Having small round shapes on it
entries.s: She wore a _____ dress that made her look like a ladybug.

entries.d: A thin piece of cloth or other stuff that holds something
entries.s: The _____ of my watch broke and it fell off.

entries.d: Having long lines of different colours
entries.s: He wore a _____ shirt that made him look like a zebra.
